data.title: Planet Morning News
Invesco’s BTCO had a net outflow of $4.8 million yesterday
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to Farside Investors data, Invesco's BTCO had a net outflow of $4.8 million yesterday.

data.title: VanEck Advisor: Tether currently holds about $5.3 billion in precious metals and $7.8 billion in Bitcoin
data.description: Gabor Gurbacs, Tether strategist and VanEck advisor, said on X platform that Tether's latest certification report shows that it holds about $5.3 billion in precious metals and $7.8 billion in Bitcoin. This may be one of the greatest commodity hedging transactions in history, which helps Tether's over-collateralization.

data.title: Hong Kong media: Half of Hong Kong's cryptocurrency exchange shops do not require real-name registration, and cash purchases of HK$120,000 or more require registration of personal information
data.description: Hong Kong media Wen Wei Po reported that half of the cryptocurrency exchange shops in Hong Kong do not require real-name registration. Reporters recently visited more than a dozen so-called cryptocurrency exchange shops (OTC) in Hong Kong and found that Tether is the most popular among all cryptocurrencies. Hong Kong still lacks supervision over OTC. About half of the exchange points allow cash to be exchanged for cryptocurrency without real-name registration. The fund tracking route is cut off from then on, and law enforcement agencies can no longer trace the whereabouts of funds. These exchange shops are currently not required to submit transaction reports to law enforcement agencies. According to a store employee who participated in KYC certification, only when purchasing cryptocurrency with cash of 120,000 Hong Kong dollars or more does it need to register personal information and take photos of customers for data retention. "Our company does not want to be involved in illegal transactions. If we do not conduct KYC certification, if someone robs money downstairs and comes up to buy cryptocurrency, we will be involved."

data.title: Canada's Bank of Montreal holds about $150 million in Bitcoin spot ETF
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The 13F-HR form (quarterly holdings report) submitted by the Bank of Montreal (BMO) of Canada to the US SEC shows that the bank held about $150 million worth of Bitcoin spot ETFs as of the end of last year, including $139 million worth of IBIT, and $11 million worth of ARKB, FBTC and GBTC.

data.title: Jupiter: To ensure transparency of token buybacks, a dashboard is planned to be launched next week
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Jupiter previously announced that it would use 50% of the protocol fees to repurchase JUP tokens and lock them for three years. In order to ensure the transparency of token repurchase transactions, Jupiter announced that it plans to launch a related dashboard next week to disclose transaction data.

data.title: BTC options worth $2.08 billion will expire today, with the biggest pain point at $98,000
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Greeks.live data shows that 21,000 BTC options will expire today, with a Put Call Ratio of 0.67, a maximum pain point of $98,000, and a nominal value of $2.08 billion. In addition, 176,000 ETH options will expire today, with a Put Call Ratio of 0.64, a maximum pain point of $2,725, and a nominal value of $470 million. Greeks.live analyst Adam said that the market remained weak this week. Although the US government issued a number of good news, the market did not buy it, and the implied volatility IV fell to the lowest level in nearly a year.
Since BTC effectively fell below the $100,000 mark, option tycoons have been continuously selling medium- and short-term call options. The trading volume of large call options has increased significantly, but the trading volume of large put options has been declining, indicating that although the market is not optimistic about the rise, the panic about the fall has also weakened. Institutions generally believe that February will be a garbage time with no market, and the market does seem to lack hot spots and funds.

data.title: ARK's ARKB had a net outflow of $52.7 million yesterday
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Farside Investors data shows that ARKB, the Bitcoin spot ETF launched by ARK, had a net outflow of US$52.7 million yesterday.

data.title: Bitwise’s BITB saw a net outflow of $15.7 million yesterday
data.description: Data from Farside Investors showed that Bitwise’s BITB saw a net outflow of $15.7 million yesterday.

data.title: Significant BTC And ETH Options Expiry Today
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from Greeks.live indicates that 21,000 Bitcoin (BTC) options are set to expire today, with a Put Call Ratio of 0.67. The maximum pain point is reported at $98,000, and the nominal value of these options is $2.08 billion.

Additionally, 176,000 Ethereum (ETH) options will also expire today, featuring a Put Call Ratio of 0.64. The maximum pain point for ETH options is $2,725, with a nominal value of $470 million.
